label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem step by step. So, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Today is her 16th birthday. We need to find the date one year ago from today in MM/DD/YYYY format. The options are given, so maybe we can check each one. First, let's get the dates straight. The last day of February is usually the 28th, unless it's a leap year, which would make it February 29th. But 2001 isn't a leap year because leap years are divisible by 4. 2000 was a leap year, then 2004, so 2001 isn't. So Jane's birthday is February 28, 2001. Now, today is her 16th birthday. That means today's date is February 28, 2017. Because 2001 + 16 = 2017. So today is 02/28/2017. We need to find the date one year ago from today. So one year before 02/28/2017 would be 02/28/2016. Wait, but 2016 is a leap year, right? Because 2016 is divisible by 4. So February 2016 had 29 days. But the question is about the date one year ago from today. So if today is 02/28/2017, then going back one year would be 02/28/2016. Even though 2016 has a leap day, the date itself is still February 28. So the answer would be 02/28/2016. Looking at the options, option C is 02/28/2016. So that's probably the correct one. Let me just double-check. Wait, if today is her 16th birthday, then 2001 + 16 is 2017. So yes, today is 02/28/2017.
